description: This API routine is obsolete. It has been replaced by the API routine two_winding_data_6, and is implemented by a call to two_winding_data_6. Use this API to modify the data of an existing two-winding transformer in the working case, or to add a new two-winding transformer to the working case (see PSSE Program Operation Manual, Owner Data). This API routine was first introduced in release 34.0.0. ierr, realaro = two_winding_data_5(ibus, jbus, ckt, intgar, realari, ratings, namear, vgrpar)
python_syntax: ierr, realaro = two_winding_data_5(ibus, jbus, ckt, intgar, realari, ratings, namear, vgrpar)

parameters.name: ibus
parameters.description: Bus number of from bus. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: jbus
parameters.description: Bus number of to bus. (input; no default allowed).

parameters.name: realari
parameters.description: An array of twenty one elementsNone. (input).

return_values.name: realaro
return_values.description: An array of two elements specifying corresponding to values REALAR(25) through REALAR(26) as described above. (output).

intgar.description: METBUS, metered end bus number (IBUS or JBUS) (IBUS by default).
O1, first owner number (owner of bus IBUS by default).
O2, second owner number (0 by default).
O3, third owner number (0 by default).
O4, fourth owner number (0 by default).
NTP1, number of tap positions (33 by default).
TAB1, impedance correction table number (0 by default).
WN1BUS, winding one side bus number (IBUS or JBUS) (IBUS by default).
CONT1, controlled bus number (0 by default).
SICOD1, negative for controlled bus on winding one side (1 by default).
COD1, adjustment control mode flag (-5 through +5) (0 by default).
CW, winding data I/O code (1 by default).
CZ, impedance data I/O code (1 by default).
CM, magnetizing admittance data I/O code (1 by default).

realari.description: R1-2, nominal transformer resistance (0.0 by default).
X1-2, nominal transformer reactance (THRSHZ by default; 0.0001 if THRSHZ = 0.0).
SBS1-2, winding base MVA (SBASE by default).
WINDV1, winding one ratio/voltage (1.0 by default if CW is 1 or 3; base voltage of bus IBUS by default if CW is 2).
NOMV1, winding one nominal voltage (0.0 by default).
1, winding one phase shift angle (0.0 by default).
WINDV2, winding two ratio/voltage (1.0 by default if CW is 1 or 3; base voltage of bus JBUS by default if CW is 2).
NOMV2, winding two nominal voltage (0.0 by default).
F1, first owner fraction (1.0 by default).
F2, second owner fraction (1.0 by default).
F3, third owner fraction (1.0 by default).
F4, fourth owner fraction (1.0 by default).
MAG1, magnetizing conductance or no-load losses (0.0 by default).
MAG2, magnetizing susceptance or exciting current (0.0 by default).
RMA1, winding one ratio/angle high limit (1.1 by default).
RMI1, winding one ratio/angle low limit (0.9 by default).
VMA1, voltage or flow upper limit (1.1 by default).
VMI1, voltage or flow lower limit (0.9 by default).
CR1, load drop compensating resistance (0.0 by default).
CX1, load drop compensating reactance (0.0 by default).
CNXA1, winding connection angle (0.0 by default). Used with adjustment control mode 5 (unsymmatric phase shift control of active power) implemented in PSSE version 32.1).

ratings.description: RATE1, rating set 1 line rating (0.0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘rate1’.
RATE2, rating set 2 line rating (0.0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘rate2’.
RATE3, rating set 3 line rating (0.0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘rate3’.
RATE4, rating set 4 line rating (0.0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘rate4’.
RATE5, rating set 5 line rating (0.0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘rate5’.
RATE6, rating set 6 line rating (0.0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘rate6’.
RATE7, rating set 7 line rating (0.0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘rate7’.
RATE8, rating set 8 line rating (0.0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘rate8’.
RATE9, rating set 9 line rating (0.0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘rate9’.
RATE10, rating set 10 line rating (0.0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘rate10’.
RATE11, rating set 11 line rating (0.0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘rate11’.
RATE12, rating set 12 line rating (0.0 by default), can also be specified with keyword ‘rate12’.

error_codes.description: circuit identifier is more than two characters.
branch exists but it is a non-transformer branch.
branch from a bus to itself.
circuit identifier may not start with &, * or @
CW, CZ and/or CM >1 but at least one endpoint bus has no base voltage.
invalid CW, CZ and/or CM value.
prerequisite requirements for API are not met.

data error, warning and information messages; one or more of: - invalid metered end bus - invalid transformer status - invalid owner number - invalid ownership fraction - no ownership data specified. - multi-section line grouping deleted - branch ownership table is full–only first n owners retained - owner table is full - invalid winding 1 side bus number - invalid CW, CZ and/or CM data value - controlled bus not found - invalid adjustment control mode flag - invalid impedance correction table number - invalid number of tap positions - winding nominal voltage is negative - winding bus base voltage and nominal voltage differ by more than 20% - invalid winding voltage - winding voltage and bus base voltage differ by more than 50% - RMAX is greater than 150% of bus base voltage - RMIN is less than 50% of bus base voltage - invalid tap ratio - tap ratio is outside the band 0.5 to 1.5 pu - RMAX is greater than 1.5 - RMIN is less than 0.5 - RMAX is less than RMIN - VMAX is less than VMIN - phase shift angle normalized between -180 and 180 - winding base MVA must be positive - MAG1 is too big relative to MAG2 at winding base MVA - magnetizing susceptance MAG2 is positive - losses are too big relative to total impedance at winding base MVA - branch reactance is 0.0 - winding 1 and winding 2 sides are interchanged (any two-terminal dc lines controlled by this transformer are logged). - winding connection angle normalized between -360 and 360 - adjustment control mode flag and the winding connection angle are inconsistent. - for asymmetric phase shifter, abnormally large or small tap setting results at RMA1 (or RMI1) and CNXA1. - for asymmetric phase shifter, the specified and derived winding tap settings differ. - vector group not found.
